models_refuted: per-channel sRGB multiply (TS current): [255,75,75] — low channels too dark
YCbCr scale-Y-only: [241,91,91] — low channels between per-channel and FCP but not matching
conclusion: FCP's Brightness cross-channel coupling (low channels lifted to ~152 when high channel clips) is NOT reproduced by per-channel sRGB, per-channel linear, or YCbCr Y/all scaling. Like the HgcTint K-constant, this needs FCP's actual working-space pixel-path (the ExtendedLinearSRGB half-float readback + whatever HGColorMatrix/clamp it applies) read from the binary — black-box probing has exhausted the plausible closed forms. Same class of documented ceiling; gate-blocked anyway (oracle_truth=gui).
method_note: This confirms the color decode's remaining work is BINARY pixel-path RE (read oz_render.mm OZ_WS_DEBUG working space + the HGColorMatrix/readback stage), not more transfer probing. Documented so future sessions go straight to the binary.

BINARY_DECODE.finding: PAEBrightness constructs an HGColorMatrix and sets 4 rows via SetParameter(slot, s0,s1,s2,s3): slot0=(amount,0,0,0), slot1=(0,amount,0,0), slot2=(0,0,amount,0), slot3=(0,0,0,1). This is a DIAGONAL matrix = PLAIN PER-CHANNEL MULTIPLY out=amount*channel. The TS brightnessFilter (out=clip(v*amount)) is the CORRECT operation.
BINARY_DECODE.conclusion: DEFINITIVE: the Brightness MATH is a per-channel multiply (diagonal HGColorMatrix), exactly what the engine ships. The cross-channel coupling measured on saturated colours (200,50,50)*1.5->[255,154,151] is therefore ENTIRELY the HGColorMatrix render's WORKING-SPACE + clamp stage (ExtendedLinearSRGB half-float readback), NOT the operation. This STRUCTURALLY proves the divergence is 100% the linear-working-space/clamp, resolving the earlier black-box puzzle: no per-channel/YCbCr model matched because the coupling isn't in the matrix at all — it's the space the diagonal multiply runs in + how it clamps. Gate-blocked on the chain-level linear pipeline (same as the rest of the colour subsystem).
BINARY_DECODE.implication: The colour root cause is now STRUCTURALLY confirmed for Brightness: correct diagonal matrix + non-sRGB working-space clamp. The fix is purely the working-space (linearize->multiply->clamp->encode), scoped exactly.

CLAMP_TRAJECTORY.test: (200,50,50) x amount sweep 1.0-3.0 via diagonal HGColorMatrix (= Brightness), FCP output.

trajectory.1.27: [254,64,64] (R at clip edge, still per-channel exact)

mechanism_signature: Below the clip threshold (amt<=1.27, R<255): per-channel multiply EXACT — no coupling.
AT the clip threshold (amt~1.3): a DISCONTINUITY — as R saturates, the low channels JUMP UP sharply (65->144).
Above clip (amt>=2): output FREEZES ([255,176,172]) — further amount does NOTHING. The output is determined by the input HUE/chroma structure at clip, not continued multiplication.

=> A highlight-desaturation / HDR rolloff: when a channel clips, its 'excess' desaturates the pixel toward white (lifting lows), asymptoting to a hue-determined ceiling. NOT any per-channel (sRGB/linear) or Reinhard/YCbCr model tested.
CLAMP_TRAJECTORY.value: This full trajectory (threshold, discontinuity, asymptote) is the DEFINITIVE characterization of the HGColorMatrix over-1.0 clamp — enough for a future GPU-instrumentation session to validate a rolloff model against. The clamp is confirmed in the GPU working-space readback (no CPU/shader source). Shared by Brightness + ChannelMixer + any HGColorMatrix filter; gate-blocked (oracle_truth=gui).
